messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following grocery delivery app privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"QuickBasket collects your name, delivery address, payment details, and order history to fulfil your grocery orders. We process this on the basis of contract performance. Order history is used to personalise product recommendations. We use Adyen for payment processing (EU-based, DPA signed). Order data is retained for 3 years. You have the right to access, correct, port, restrict, erase, and object to your data by emailing privacy@quickbasket.com. Our DPO is at dpo@quickbasket.com. We store data in EU data centres only. We notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours of a confirmed breach and customers without undue delay."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) stated for order fulfilment. Personalised recommendations is a secondary purpose that requires its own lawful basis — this is not separately addressed.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Order history can reveal dietary requirements related to religion or health. No explicit safeguard, though this is a low-risk inference at scale.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] Order fulfilment and personalised recommendations are both stated, but only one lawful basis is provided.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 3-year order data retention clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PASS] Access (Art. 15), rectification (Art. 16), erasure (Art. 17), restriction (Art. 18), portability (Art. 20), and objection (Art. 21) all listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Adyen named as EU-based payment processor with signed DPA.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] EU data centres confirmed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO contact at dpo@quickbasket.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour supervisory authority notification and customer notification without undue delay both stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (personalised recommendations lack separate lawful basis), Art. 9 (dietary/religious inference risk from order history not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State a separate lawful basis for personalised recommendations (legitimate interest with LIA, or opt-in consent)
- Acknowledge that order history may reveal dietary preferences linked to religion or health
